perturbation.original_text: 9. Standstill. Each of the parties agrees that, until the expiration of eighteen months from the date of this Agreement, without the prior written consent of the Board of Directors or General Partners of the other party, it and its affiliates will not (i) in any manner acquire, agree to acquire or make any proposal or offer or otherwise seek to acquire, directly or indirectly any securities (or rights in respect thereof), assets or property of the other party or any of its subsidiaries, whether such agreements or proposals or offers are made with or to the other party (or any controlling person or successor thereof) or any of its subsidiaries
perturbation.changed_text: 9. Standstill. Each of the parties agrees that, until the expiration of eighteen months from the date of this Agreement, without the prior written consent of the Board of Directors or General Partners of the other party, it and its affiliates will not (i) in any manner acquire, agree to acquire or make any proposal or offer or otherwise seek to acquire, directly or indirectly any securities (or rights in respect thereof), assets or property of the other party or any of its subsidiaries, whether such agreements or proposals or offers are made with or to the other party (or any controlling person or successor thereof) or any of its subsidiaries, unless explicitly permitted by Section 9(a)
perturbation.explanation: The change introduces an in-text contradiction. Section 9 initially prohibits certain actions (acquiring securities, assets, etc.) for 18 months. The addition of 'unless explicitly permitted by Section 9(a)' creates a contradiction, as there is no Section 9(a), which creates uncertainty about the enforcement of the standstill agreement. This change creates an in-text contradiction, by making it unclear whether the 18-month restriction is absolute or conditional, leading to a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Standstill. Each of the parties agrees that, until the expiration of eighteen months from the date of this Agreement, without the prior written consent of the Board of Directors or General Partners of the other party, it and its affiliates will not (i) in any manner acquire, agree to acquire or make any proposal or offer or otherwise seek to acquire, directly or indirectly any securities (or rights in respect thereof), assets or property of the other party or any of its subsidiaries, whether such agreements or proposals or offers are made with or to the other party (or any controlling person or successor thereof) or any of its subsidiaries, unless explicitly permitted by Section 9(a); (ii) enter into or agree, offer, seek or propose to enter into or otherwise be involved in or part of, directly or indirectly, any merger, acquisition transaction or other business combination involving the other party or any of its subsidiaries or any of their respective assets; (iii) make, or in any way participate in, directly or indirectly, any "solicitation" of "proxies" (as such terms are used in the proxy rules of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the "Exchange Act")) to vote, or seek to advise or influence any person with respect to the voting of, any voting securities of the other party or any of its subsidiaries; (iv) form, join or in any way participate in a "group" (within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Exchange Act) with respect to any voting securities of the other party or any of its subsidiaries; (v) otherwise act, alone or in concert with others, to seek or propose to control or influence the management, Board of Directors or policies of the other party; (vi) directly or indirectly enter into any discussions, negotiations, arrangements or understandings with any other person with respect to any of the foregoing activities or propose any of such activities to 3 any other person; or (vii) publicly disclose any intention, plan or arrangement inconsistent with the foregoing; (viii) directly or indirectly advise, encourage, provide assistance (including debt or equity financial assistance) to or hold discussions with or invest in any other person in connection with any of the foregoing.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 9 established a standstill agreement with a clear 18-month restriction. The changed text adds 'unless explicitly permitted by Section 9(a),' but no Section 9(a) exists. This creates a contradiction because the standstill agreement is now conditional on a non-existent provision, making the restriction ambiguous and potentially unenforceable. The contradicted text is the same section with the added text. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: 1. Non-Disclosure of Confidential Information. (a) Each of PictureTel and Polycom shall (i) use the Confidential Information obtained from the other solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction and for no competitive or other purpose; (ii) not disclose the Confidential Information to any third party, except for disclosures to its directors, executive officers and representatives of its advisors (such as independent accountants, investment bankers, consultants and attorneys) acting on its behalf (collectively, its "Representatives") who in each case, in such party's reasonable judgment, need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction and who are set forth on Annex A hereto, as it may be amended by the parties from time to time
perturbation.changed_text: 1. Non-Disclosure of Confidential Information. (a) Each of PictureTel and Polycom shall (i) use the Confidential Information obtained from the other solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction and for commercial purposes; (ii) not disclose the Confidential Information to any third party, except for disclosures to its directors, executive officers and representatives of its advisors (such as independent accountants, investment bankers, consultants and attorneys) acting on its behalf (collectively, its "Representatives") who in each case, in such party's reasonable judgment, need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction and who are set forth on Annex A hereto, as it may be amended by the parties from time to time
perturbation.explanation: The change introduces an in-text contradiction. Originally the clause states the Confidential Information obtained from the other must be use solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible transaction and for no other competitive or other purpose. The change now states that it can be used for 'commercial purposes'. This creates contradiction within this section because a competitive purpose falls within commercial purposes, therefore the terms contradict with each other making it confusing whether the confidential information can be used for a competitive purpose.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Non-Disclosure of Confidential Information. (a) Each of PictureTel and Polycom shall (i) use the Confidential Information obtained from the other solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction and for commercial purposes; (ii) not disclose the Confidential Information to any third party, except for disclosures to its directors, executive officers and representatives of its advisors (such as independent accountants, investment bankers, consultants and attorneys) acting on its behalf (collectively, its "Representatives") who in each case, in such party's reasonable judgment, need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction and who are set forth on Annex A hereto, as it may be amended by the parties from time to time; (iii) inform its Representatives of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and direct its Representatives to treat the Confidential Information confidentially and in accordance with the terms of this Agreement; (iv) take all additional reasonable precautions necessary to prevent the disclosure of the Confidential Information by its Representatives to any third party; and (v) be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies section 1. (a) (i) to allow the use of confidential information for 'commercial purposes' in addition to evaluating a possible transaction. The original text only allowed it to be used for evaluating a transaction. The contradiction arises because 'commercial purposes' could include competitive purposes, which was explicitly disallowed in the original text. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, indicating that the change itself creates a conflict within that section by broadening the permitted uses of confidential information in a way that conflicts with the implicit restriction against competitive use, making it unclear whether using it for competitive purposes is allowed.

perturbation.original_text: 6. No Agreement. Unless a definitive agreement regarding a Transaction between PictureTel and Polycom has been executed and delivered, neither Polycom, PictureTel nor any of their stockholders or affiliates will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such a Transaction by virtue of this letter agreement or any other written or oral expression with respect to such Transaction except, in the case of this Agreement, matters specifically agreed to herein. Each party 2 further acknowledges and agrees that each party reserves the right, in its sole discretion, to reject any and all proposals made by the other party or any of its Representatives with regard to a Transaction, to deny any request for Confidential Information (regardless of the reasonableness of such request) and to terminate discussions or negotiations with the other party at any time.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. No Agreement. Unless a definitive agreement regarding a Transaction between PictureTel and Polycom has been executed and delivered, neither Polycom, PictureTel nor any of their stockholders or affiliates will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such a Transaction by virtue of this letter agreement or any other written or oral expression with respect to such Transaction except, in the case of this Agreement, matters specifically agreed to herein. Each party 2 further acknowledges and agrees that each party reserves the right, in its sole discretion, to reject any and all proposals made by the other party or any of its Representatives with regard to a Transaction, to deny any request for Confidential Information (regardless of the reasonableness of such request) and to terminate discussions or negotiations with the other party at any time, provided they give 30 days written notice.
perturbation.explanation: The change introduces an in-text contradiction. Section 6 gives each party the right to terminate discussions or negotiations at any time. The added clause 'provided they give 30 days written notice' contradicts the 'at any time' clause because termination is no longer instant. The text now contains an in-text contradiction concerning the right to 'terminate discussions or negotiations', as it is now unclear whether termination can be immediate or requires a 30-day waiting period.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. No Agreement. Unless a definitive agreement regarding a Transaction between PictureTel and Polycom has been executed and delivered, neither Polycom, PictureTel nor any of their stockholders or affiliates will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such a Transaction by virtue of this letter agreement or any other written or oral expression with respect to such Transaction except, in the case of this Agreement, matters specifically agreed to herein. Each party 2 further acknowledges and agrees that each party reserves the right, in its sole discretion, to reject any and all proposals made by the other party or any of its Representatives with regard to a Transaction, to deny any request for Confidential Information (regardless of the reasonableness of such request) and to terminate discussions or negotiations with the other party at any time, provided they give 30 days written notice. Neither this paragraph nor any other provision in this Agreement can be waived or amended except by written consent of PictureTel and Polycom, which consent shall refer specifically to this paragraph (or such other provision) and explicitly make such waiver or amendment.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 6 allows termination of discussions 'at any time'. The changed text modifies this by adding 'provided they give 30 days written notice'. This directly contradicts the 'at any time' clause, as termination is no longer immediate but requires a 30-day waiting period. Since the changed text and contradicted text are the same, it is a clear in-text contradiction as it is now unclear whether termination can be immediate or requires a 30-day waiting period, which aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Non-Public Information. PictureTel has outstanding publicly-held securities and the Confidential Information contains material non-public information. Each of the parties acknowledges that it is (i) aware, and has advised or will advise its Representatives, that the United States securities laws prohibit any person in possession of material non-public information about a company from purchasing or selling securities of such company, and from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person may purchase or sell such securities and (ii) familiar with the Securities and Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, and the rules and regulations thereunder, and each party agrees that it will neither use nor permit any of its Representatives to use any Confidential Information in violation of such Act or rules or regulations, including, without limitation, Rules 10b-5 and 14e-3.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Non-Public Information. PictureTel has outstanding publicly-held securities and the Confidential Information contains material non-public information. Each of the parties acknowledges that it is (i) aware, and has advised or will advise its Representatives, that the United States securities laws prohibit any person in possession of material non-public information about a company from purchasing or selling securities of such company, and from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person may purchase or sell such securities, except if pre-approved by legal counsel; and (ii) familiar with the Securities and Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, and the rules and regulations thereunder, and each party agrees that it will neither use nor permit any of its Representatives to use any Confidential Information in violation of such Act or rules or regulations, including, without limitation, Rules 10b-5 and 14e-3.
perturbation.explanation: The changed text introduces an in-text contradiction. Initially, the clause states that the United States securities laws prohibit any person in possession of material non-public information about a company from purchasing or selling securities of such company, and from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person may purchase or sell such securities. With the addition of 'except if pre-approved by legal counsel', it contradicts the initial prohibition, by now allowing such actions under the condition of approval from legal counsel. This creates ambiguity and in-text contradiction as to what should be followed, the United States security laws or the mentioned exception within the text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Non-Public Information. PictureTel has outstanding publicly-held securities and the Confidential Information contains material non-public information. Each of the parties acknowledges that it is (i) aware, and has advised or will advise its Representatives, that the United States securities laws prohibit any person in possession of material non-public information about a company from purchasing or selling securities of such company, and from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person may purchase or sell such securities, except if pre-approved by legal counsel; and (ii) familiar with the Securities and Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, and the rules and regulations thereunder, and each party agrees that it will neither use nor permit any of its Representatives to use any Confidential Information in violation of such Act or rules or regulations, including, without limitation, Rules 10b-5 and 14e-3.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 8 introduces a direct contradiction by adding 'except if pre-approved by legal counsel' to the prohibition against using non-public information for securities trading. This creates an exception that conflicts with the initial, absolute prohibition stated in the original text, and therefore also the contradicted text, creating a clear inconsistency within the document as to whether such trading is allowed or not. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type, because the modified text creates conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 2. Non-Disclosure of Negotiations or Agreements. Except as required by law, neither PictureTel, Polycom nor their respective Representatives shall disclose to any person (including, without limitation, any director, officer or employee that is not set forth on Annex A) the existence, status or terms of any discussions, negotiations or agreements between them concerning a possible Transaction, including without limitation any offer, letter of intent, proposal, price, value or valuation, or any similar terms, agreements or understandings between PictureTel and Polycom with respect thereto, or that such party has provided to or received from the other Confidential Information, without obtaining the prior written consent of the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Non-Disclosure of Negotiations or Agreements. Except as required by law, neither PictureTel, Polycom nor their respective Representatives shall disclose to any person (including, without limitation, any director, officer or employee that is not set forth on Annex A) the existence, status or terms of any discussions, negotiations or agreements between them concerning a possible Transaction, including without limitation any offer, letter of intent, proposal, price, value or valuation, or any similar terms, agreements or understandings between PictureTel and Polycom with respect thereto, or that such party has provided to or received from the other Confidential Information, without obtaining the prior written consent of the other party, except for any disclosures to close family members.
perturbation.explanation: The change introduces an in-text contradiction. Section 2 originally prohibits disclosure of negotiations or agreements. With the addition of 'except for any disclosures to close family members', the terms in the same section now contradict each other. Disclosing such information is not allowed in the initial clause, however with the change, the terms are being contradicted because a party can now disclose it to family members, creating a contradiction in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Non-Disclosure of Negotiations or Agreements. Except as required by law, neither PictureTel, Polycom nor their respective Representatives shall disclose to any person (including, without limitation, any director, officer or employee that is not set forth on Annex A) the existence, status or terms of any discussions, negotiations or agreements between them concerning a possible Transaction, including without limitation any offer, letter of intent, proposal, price, value or valuation, or any similar terms, agreements or understandings between PictureTel and Polycom with respect thereto, or that such party has provided to or received from the other Confidential Information, without obtaining the prior written consent of the other party, except for any disclosures to close family members. The term "person" as used in this Agreement will be interpreted broadly to include the media and any corporation, company, group, partnership or other entity or individual. 3. Ownership of Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 2 to allow disclosures to close family members, while the original intent of the section was to prevent any unauthorized disclosure, as the original text states 'neither PictureTel, Polycom nor their respective Representatives shall disclose to any person...'. This alteration directly contradicts the confidentiality obligations outlined in both the changed text and the contradicted text, as now some disclosures are permitted while the original intent was to prohibit all such disclosures without prior written consent. 'Person' is also defined broadly. This inconsistency creates an in-text contradiction, aligning with the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.
